title: building ubuntu.22.04 based image fails on dpkg call when using fuse_overlayfs
body:
### Issue Description

Building the following Container which install openssh-client

```Containerfile
FROM docker.io/ubuntu:22.04
RUN apt-get -y update
RUN apt-get install -y openssh-client
# Workaround
# RUN apt-get -o Debug::pkgDPkgPM=true install -y openssh-client
```

fails with 

```
Errors were encountered while processing:
 openssh-client
W: Download is performed unsandboxed as root as file '/var/cache/apt/archives/partial/libmd0_1.0.4-1build1_amd64.deb' couldn't be accessed by user '_apt'. - pkgAcquire::Run (13: Permission denied)
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
Error: building at STEP "RUN apt-get install -y openssh-client": while running runtime: exit status 100
```

For some reason the workaround shown in the Containerfile above fixes the problem.

Furthermore I can only reproduce the issue when using `mount_program=fuse-overlayfs` in `storage.conf`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf` to 
```toml
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  graphroot = "/data/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
  mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
where `/data` is mounted with autofs
2. Build following container with `podman build --no-cache .`
```Containerfile
FROM docker.io/ubuntu:22.04
RUN apt-get -y update
RUN apt-get install -y openssh-client
```
produces the failure.


### Describe the results you received

Building the image exits with
```
Errors were encountered while processing:
 openssh-client
W: Download is performed unsandboxed as root as file '/var/cache/apt/archives/partial/libmd0_1.0.4-1build1_amd64.deb' couldn't be accessed by user '_apt'. - pkgAcquire::Run (13: Permission denied)
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
Error: building at STEP "RUN apt-get install -y openssh-client": while running runtime: exit status 100
```

### Describe the results you expected

Successful build image.

comments.body:
I don't know what the latest status on this is but running your container storage on top of nfs is not fully supported as you can see by the warning:
`WARN[0000] Network file system detected as backing store.  Enforcing overlay option `force_mask="700"`.  Add it to storage.conf to silence this warning `

Also see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-nfs

cc @rhatdan @giuseppe
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This looks like not a bug, but the code handled it correctly.
